question: What is the purpose of vector embeddings in a large language model (LLM)?

options.A: Splitting text into manageable pieces of data
options.B: Grouping a set of characters to be treated as a single unit
options.C: Providing the ability to mathematically compare texts
options.D: Providing the count of every word in the input

explanation: C. Providing the ability to mathematically compare texts: This is the correct purpose. Vector embeddings map text into a high-dimensional space where the distance or proximity between vectors reflects the semantic similarity of the original texts. If two words, sentences, or documents have similar meanings, their corresponding embeddings will be closer together in this vector space. This allows for mathematical operations (like calculating cosine similarity) to quantify how related two pieces of text are, which is crucial for tasks like semantic search, recommendation systems, clustering, and understanding context.

question:
A company wants to fine-tune a foundation model (FM) by using AWS services. The company needs to ensure that
its data stays private, safe, and secure in the source AWS Region where the data is stored.
Which combination of steps will meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively? (Choose two.)

options.A: Host the model on premises by using AWS Outposts.
options.C: Use AWS PrivateLink and a VPC.
options.D: Host the Amazon Bedrock API on premises.
options.E: Use Amazon CloudWatch logs and metrics.

explanation:
B. Use the Amazon Bedrock API.

Cost-effectiveness: Amazon Bedrock is a fully managed service that provides access to foundation models (FMs) from Amazon and leading AI companies through a single API. This means you don't have to provision, manage, or scale the underlying GPU infrastructure for training or inference of these large models. You pay only for what you use (inference calls, provisioned throughput), which is significantly more cost-effective than building and maintaining your own generative AI infrastructure, especially for companies that don't specialize in AI model operations.

C. Use AWS PrivateLink and a VPC.

Cost-effectiveness (for secure/private access): AWS PrivateLink allows you to connect your Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) directly to AWS services (like Amazon Bedrock) without traversing the public internet.

This provides enhanced security, compliance, and network performance (lower latency).

Compared to routing traffic over the public internet, PrivateLink can be more cost-effective for large volumes of sensitive data due to reduced data transfer costs for VPC endpoint traffic (which often stays within the AWS network) and the elimination of the need for complex, expensive network security appliances or VPNs to achieve the same level of private connectivity. It simplifies network architecture, reducing operational overhead.

question: Which metric is used to evaluate the performance of foundation models (FMs) for text summarization tasks?
explanation:
B. Bilingual Evaluation Understudy (BLEU) score.

The BLEU (Bilingual Evaluation Understudy) score is a metric originally designed for machine translation, but it is widely applied to text summarization.

N-gram Overlap: It calculates the similarity between a machine-generated summary and a "reference" summary (usually written by a human). It looks for sequences of words (n-grams) that appear in both.

Precision-Oriented: BLEU measures how much of the machine-generated text is present in the reference text.

A higher score (closer to 1.0 or 100%) indicates that the model's summary closely matches the professional human summary.

Efficiency: It provides an automated, objective way to evaluate thousands of summaries without requiring a human to read every single one.

Note: In many AWS and AI contexts, the ROUGE (Recall-Oriented Understudy for Gisting Evaluation) score is considered the "gold standard" for summarization because it focuses on Recall (ensuring all key points from the reference are included). However, among the choices provided, BLEU is the standard metric used for text- to-text similarity.

question:
A company has set up a translation tool to help its customer service team handle issues from customers around
the world. The company wants to evaluate the performance of the translation tool. The company sets up a parallel
data process that compares the responses from the tool to responses from actual humans. Both sets of responses
are generated on the same set of documents.
Which strategy should the company use to evaluate the translation tool?

options.A: Use the Bilingual Evaluation Understudy (BLEU) score to estimate the absolute translation quality of the two methods.
options.B: Use the Bilingual Evaluation Understudy (BLEU) score to estimate the relative translation quality of the two methods.
options.C: Use the BERTScore to estimate the absolute translation quality of the two methods.
options.D: Use the BERTScore to estimate the relative translation quality of the two methods.

explanation:
B. Use the Bilingual Evaluation Understudy (BLEU) score to estimate the relative translation quality of the two methods.

Relative vs. Absolute Quality: When evaluating a new tool against an existing one, the goal is to determine which performs better. This is a relative comparison, not an absolute one. An absolute score would require a human-defined baseline of perfection, which is difficult to automate and measure with a single metric. The BLEU score, by its nature, is best used for comparing the output of two or more different machine translation systems to determine which one is superior.

The BLEU Score: The Bilingual Evaluation Understudy (BLEU) score is a classic and widely used metric for evaluating the quality of machine-translated text. It works by comparing the machine-generated translation to one or more high-quality human reference translations. The score is based on the overlap of n-grams (sequences of words) and a penalty for short sentences. A higher BLEU score indicates a closer match to the human reference translations. This makes it ideal for the scenario of comparing a new method against an existing one to see if it produces translations that are more aligned with human expectations.

question:
An AI practitioner wants to generate more diverse and more creative outputs from a large language model (LLM).
How should the AI practitioner adjust the inference parameter?
explanation:
A. Increase the temperature value.

Temperature is an inference parameter that controls the randomness and creativity of an LLM's output. A higher temperature value (e.g., closer to 1 or higher) increases the probability of selecting less likely tokens (words or parts of words). This makes the generated text more random, surprising, and diverse. A lower temperature value (e.g., closer to 0) makes the model more deterministic and predictable, causing it to choose the most probable tokens and resulting in more focused and less creative outputs.

question:
A company is integrating AI into its employee recruitment and hiring solution. The company wants to mitigate bias
risks and ensure responsible AI practices while prioritizing equitable hiring decisions.
Which core dimensions of responsible AI should the company consider? (Choose two.)
explanation:
**Fairness**

Fairness is a critical dimension of responsible AI, especially in a high-stakes application like hiring. It ensures that the AI system does not discriminate against individuals or groups based on protected characteristics such as race, gender, age, or religion. In the context of a recruitment solution, fairness means the model's hiring decisions are not biased by historical data that might contain human prejudices. For example, if past hiring decisions favored a specific demographic, an AI model trained on that data could perpetuate this bias.

To mitigate this, the company must actively work to ensure the model makes equitable decisions.

**Transparency**

Transparency (also known as explainability) is about understanding how the AI system arrives at its decisions.

In an AI hiring solution, transparency would allow the company to see why a candidate was ranked highly or disqualified. This is crucial for building trust, for auditing the system for bias, and for complying with regulations. A transparent system allows human recruiters to review the decision-making process, ensuring that the AI is not acting as a "black box" and that candidates are evaluated on justifiable criteria like skills and

**experience, not hidden biases**

question:
An education company is building a chatbot whose target audience is teenagers. The company is training a custom

explanation:
D. Bilingual Evaluation Understudy (BLEU) score.

BLEU score is the most suitable metric here because it's designed to evaluate the quality of a generated text by comparing it to one or more reference texts. While it was originally developed for machine translation, its core principle—measuring n-gram overlap and brevity—makes it highly effective for this specific use case.

The company wants the chatbot to produce creative spellings and shortened words that mimic teenage slang.

These creative spellings and shortened words can be treated as "translations" into a specific linguistic style.

question:
A customer service team is developing an application to analyze customer feedback and automatically classify the
feedback into different categories. The categories include product quality, customer service, and delivery
experience.
Which A1 concept does this scenario present?
explanation:
B. Natural language processing (NLP).

Natural language processing (NLP) is a field of AI that focuses on enabling computers to understand, interpret, and generate human language. In this scenario, the application is analyzing customer feedback, which is unstructured text data. The core task is to read this feedback and automatically sort it into predefined categories. This process, known as text classification or categorization, is a fundamental application of NLP.

question:
A financial services company must ensure that its generative AI-powered chatbot provides factual responses for
regulatory compliance.
Which solution prevents the underlying foundation model (FM) from hallucinating?

options.A: Use AWS Config to query compliance metadata by using natural language.
options.B: Configure Amazon Bedrock Guardrails to evaluate user inputs and model responses.
options.C: Use Amazon Fraud Detector to detect potentially fraudulent online activities.
options.D: Use AWS Audit Manager to prepare IT audit and compliance reports.

explanation:
B. Configure Amazon Bedrock Guardrails to evaluate user inputs and model responses.

The issue here is hallucination — when a foundation model (FM) generates responses that are not factual.

Amazon Bedrock Guardrails allow you to enforce policies, filter content, and validate responses. You can configure rules so that the model either grounds its responses in a knowledge base or rejects unsafe/unverified outputs, which directly addresses the compliance requirement.

question:
A company wants to develop a solution that uses generative AI to create content for product advertisements,
including sample images and slogans.
Select the correct model type from the following list for each action. Each model type should be selected one time.
explanation:
1. Create high-quality images that are influenced by the generated slogans and product Diffusion models (like Stable Diffusion, DALL·E, Imagen) are state-of-the-art for generating realistic, high- quality images from text or guidance prompts.

They are ideal for this task since the slogan (text) influences the image creation.

Answer: Diffusion model.

**2. Create contextually relevant slogans based on the advertisement product**

Transformer-based models (like GPT, BERT) are powerful at handling natural language tasks such as text generation, summarization, and contextual understanding.

Generating slogans from product descriptions is purely an NLP task → Transformers are best.

Answer: Transformer-based model.

**3. Ensure that company brand elements are properly placed in the images**

This is about detecting objects/visual elements (logos, brand assets) and verifying their position in generated images.

Object detection models (YOLO, Faster R-CNN) specialize in identifying and locating objects within images.

Perfect for checking if logos/brand icons are present and properly placed.

Answer: Object detection model.
items: Create high-quality images that are influenced by the generated slogans and product
Create contextually relevant slogans based on the advertisement product
Ensure that company brand elements are properly placed in the images

question:
A company has created multiple ML models. The company needs a solution for storing, managing, and versioning
the models.
Which AWS service or feature meets these requirements?
explanation: C. Amazon SageMaker Model Registry → Purpose-built to catalog, version, approve, and manage ML models for deployment and compliance. Perfect for governance and lifecycle management.

question:
An AI practitioner is building an ML model. The AI practitioner wants to provide model transparency and
explainability to stakeholders.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.D: Provide a secure model inference endpoint.

explanation: A. Present the model Shapley values → Shapley values (SHAP) provide explainability, showing how each feature contributes to a prediction. This meets regulatory and interpretability requirements.

question:
A company is developing an ML application. The application must automatically group similar customers and
products based on their characteristics. Which ML strategy should the company use to meet these requirements?
explanation: Unsupervised learning → Used when the data has no predefined labels. The model identifies patterns, similarities, and groupings (e.g., customer segmentation, product clustering).

question:
A news agency publishes articles in English. The agency wants to make articles available in other languages.
Which solution meets these requirements?

options.A: Add Amazon Transcribe to the company’s website.
options.B: Use the Amazon Translate real-time translation feature.
options.C: Add Amazon Personalize to the company’s website.
options.D: Use the Amazon Textract real-time document processing feature.

explanation: B. Amazon Translate → A neural machine translation (NMT) service for real-time and batch translation between languages. Perfect fit for publishing articles in multiple languages.

question:
A bank is building a chatbot to answer customer questions about opening a bank account. The chatbot will use
public bank documents to generate responses. The company will use Amazon Bedrock and prompt engineering to
improve the chatbot’s responses.
Which prompt engineering technique meets these requirements?
explanation: C. Few-shot prompting → You provide the model with a few examples of the task (e.g., input → output pairs), so it can infer the desired format, tone, or reasoning style. This improves accuracy and consistency.

question:
A company wants to fine-tune an ML model that is hosted on Amazon Bedrock. The company wants to use its own
sensitive data that is stored in private databases in a VPC. The data needs to stay within the company’s private
network.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Restrict access to Amazon Bedrock by using an AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) service role.
options.B: Restrict access to Amazon Bedrock by using an AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) resource policy.
options.C: Use AWS PrivateLink to connect the VPC and Amazon Bedrock.
options.D: Use AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) keys to encrypt the data.

explanation: C. AWS PrivateLink → Provides secure private connectivity between VPCs and AWS services without using the public internet. This ensures the sensitive data stays within the private network when connecting to Bedrock.

question:
A documentary filmmaker wants to reach more viewers. The filmmaker wants to automatically add subtitles and
voice-overs in multiple languages to their films.
Which combination of steps will meet these requirements? (Choose two.)

options.A: Use Amazon Transcribe and Amazon Translate to generate subtitles in other languages.
options.B: Use Amazon Textract and Amazon Translate to generate subtitles in other languages.
options.C: Use Amazon Polly to generate voice-overs in other languages.
options.D: Use Amazon Translate to generate voice-overs in other languages.
options.E: Use Amazon Textract to generate voice-overs in other languages.

explanation:
**Generate subtitles in other languages**

Start with Amazon Transcribe → converts speech (from video/audio) into text.

Then use Amazon Translate → converts the transcribed text into other languages.

That’s exactly A.

**Generate voice-overs in other languages**

Amazon Polly → converts translated text into natural-sounding speech. Perfect for multilingual voice-overs.

**(C)**

question:
A company wants to create a chatbot to answer employee questions about company policies. Company policies are
updated frequently. The chatbot must reflect the changes in near real time. The company wants to choose a large
language model (LLM).
Which solution meets these requirements?

options.A: Fine-tune an LLM on the company policy text by using Amazon SageMaker.
options.B: Select a foundation model (FM) from Amazon Bedrock to build an application.
options.C: Create a Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) workflow by using Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases.
options.D: Use Amazon Q Business to build a custom Q App.

explanation: C. Create a Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) workflow by using Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases This allows the model to retrieve the latest company policy documents at query time and generate grounded, accurate answers. This is the best and most cost-effective approach.

question:
A company is using supervised learning to train an AI model on a small labeled dataset that is specific to a target
task.
Which step of the foundation model (FM) lifecycle does this describe?
explanation: A. Fine-tuning → The process of taking a pre-trained foundation model and training it further with a small, task-specific labeled dataset. This exactly matches the description.

question:
A company is developing an AI application to help the company approve or deny personal loans. The application
must follow the principles of responsible AI.
Select the correct responsible AI principle from the following list for each action. Select each responsible AI
principle one time or not at all.
explanation:
1. Encrypt the application data, and isolate the application on a private network.

This ensures sensitive information is protected and that access is restricted.

This aligns with Privacy and Security (protecting data confidentiality, integrity, and access).

**Answer: Privacy and security**

2. Evaluate how different population groups will be impacted.

This is about checking whether the model treats people fairly regardless of demographic characteristics (age, gender, ethnicity, etc.).

The principle here is Fairness → preventing bias and discrimination.

**Answer: Fairness**

3. Test the application with unexpected data to ensure the application will work in unique situations.

This is about how well the system can handle edge cases, anomalies, or conditions outside of training data.

That aligns with Robustness → making the model resilient and reliable under varying conditions.

**Answer: Robustness**
items: Encrypt the application data, and isolate the application on a private network
Evaluate how different population groups will be impacted
Test the application with unexpected data to ensure the application will work in unique situations

question:
A company is introducing a new feature for its application. The feature will refine the style of output messages.
The company will fine-tune a large language model (LLM) on Amazon Bedrock to implement the feature.
Which type of data does the company need to meet these requirements?

options.C: Samples of pairs of input and output messages
options.D: Separate samples of input and output messages

explanation: C. Samples of pairs of input and output messages → Correct, because fine-tuning is supervised and requires input-output pairs.

question:
A healthcare company is building an AI solution to predict patient readmission within 30 days of patient discharge.
The company has trained a model on historical patient data including medical history, demographics, and
treatment specifications, to provide readmission predictions in real time.
Which task describes AI model inference in this scenario?

options.B: Use appropriate metrics and assess model performance.
options.C: Use data to identify patient patterns and correlations.
options.D: Use a trained model to predict patient readmission.

explanation: D. Use a trained model to predict patient readmission → Exactly what inference means: applying the trained model in real time for predictions.

question:
A financial company wants to build workflows for human review of ML predictions. The company wants to define
confidence thresholds for its use case and adjust the thresholds over time.
Which AWS service meets these requirements?
explanation:
**B. Amazon Augmented AI (Amazon A2I)**

The company needs:

**Human review of ML predictions**

**Confidence thresholds (define when predictions need human oversight)**

**Ability to adjust thresholds over time**

This is exactly the use case for Amazon Augmented AI (A2I).

question:
A company wants to develop an AI assistant for employees to query internal data.
Which AWS service will meet this requirement?
explanation: D. Amazon Q Business → A generative AI-powered assistant designed specifically for employees to query company data, documents, and applications securely.

question:
A company wants to build and deploy ML models on AWS without writing any code.
Which AWS service or feature meets these requirements?
explanation: Amazon SageMaker Canvas is a visual, no-code ML interface that allows business analysts and other non- technical users to: . Build ML models without writing any code. . Generate predictions using a point-and-click interface. . Use built-in data preparation and visualization features. . Access models trained with AutoML behind the scenes.

question:
A design company is using a foundation model (FM) on Amazon Bedrock to generate images for various projects.
The company wants to have control over how detailed or abstract each generated image appears
Which model parameter should the company modify?
explanation:
C. Generation step → In image generation (especially diffusion models), the number of inference/generation steps controls how much refinement is applied: Fewer steps → faster, more abstract, less detail.

More steps → slower, but more refined and detailed images.

question:
A financial company has offices in different countries worldwide. The company requires that all API calls between
generative AI applications and foundation models (FM) must not travel across the public internet.
Which AWS service should the company use?
explanation: A. AWS PrivateLink → Provides secure, private connectivity between VPCs and AWS services without exposing traffic to the public internet. Perfect for Bedrock or other AWS service calls from within private networks.

question:
An ecommerce company is deploying a chatbot. The chatbot will give users the ability to ask questions about the
company’s products and receive details on users’ orders. The company must implement safeguards for the chatbot
to filter harmful content from the input prompts and chatbot responses.
Which AWS feature or resource meets these requirements?
explanation:
A. Amazon Bedrock Guardrails → Purpose-built for adding safeguards to foundation model applications.

Guardrails can: Block harmful or policy-violating user inputs.

Filter or redact unsafe model outputs.

question:
A company wants to learn about generative AI applications in an experimental environment.
Which solution will meet this requirement MOST cost-effectively?
explanation: C. Amazon Bedrock PartyRock → A free, no-code playground built on Amazon Bedrock that allows users to easily experiment with generative AI apps. This is explicitly designed for learning and experimenting at low/no cost.

question:
A company needs to collect a large dataset to train an AI assistant in a specific content area.
Which dataset will meet this requirement?

options.A: Diverse conversations that use relevant terminology
options.B: Time series data of general purpose historical sales
options.D: Unique product IDs and corresponding user IDs

explanation: A. Diverse conversations that use relevant terminology → Exactly what’s needed: conversations in the domain with the right language and context.

question:
A financial company is developing a generative AI application for loan approval decisions. The company needs the
application output to be responsible and fair.
Which solution meets these requirements?

options.A: Review the training data to check for biases. Include data from all demographics in the training data.
options.B: Use a deep learning model with many hidden layers.
options.C: Keep the model’s decision-making process a secret to protect proprietary algorithms.
options.D: Continuously monitor the model’s performance on a static test dataset

explanation:
A. Review the training data to check for biases. Include data from all demographics in the training data.

This ensures the model does not underrepresent or discriminate against specific groups.

Directly addresses fairness and ethical AI.

question:
Select the correct AWS service or tool from the following list for each use case. Select each AWS service or tool
one time or not at all.
explanation:
1. Apply human feedback across the ML lifecycle to improve the accuracy and relevancy of models

**Answer: Amazon SageMaker Ground Truth**

Ground Truth allows you to build and manage human-in-the-loop (HITL) workflows for labeling training data and model outputs.

Human feedback ensures higher accuracy, relevance, and continuous improvement of ML models.

**2. Implement safeguards that align with responsible AI policies**

**Answer: Amazon Bedrock Guardrails**

Guardrails help control user inputs and model responses, filtering harmful, toxic, or policy-violating content.

Ensures AI applications stay safe, ethical, and policy-compliant.

**3. Detect potential bias during data preparation and model training**

**Answer: Amazon SageMaker Clarify**

Clarify provides bias detection in datasets and models, and explainability for predictions.
items: Apply human feedback across the ML lifecycle to improve the accuracy and relevancy of models
Implement safeguards that align with responsible AI policies
Detect potential bias during data preparation and model training

question:
An AI practitioner who has minimal ML knowledge wants to predict employee attrition without writing code.
Which Amazon SageMaker feature meets this requirement?
explanation: A. SageMaker Canvas → A no-code ML interface that lets users build ML models using a visual interface (drag-and-drop) to make predictions such as employee attrition. Perfect fit.

question:
A company is using AI to improve its services. The company needs to ensure that the AI system is fair and
explainable. The company wants to require training for members of the AI system development team.
Which training will meet these requirements?

options.B: Training on data privacy and encryption protocols
options.C: Training on bias awareness and responsible AI

explanation: C. Training on bias awareness and responsible AI → Directly addresses fairness (avoiding bias) and responsible AI principles, making this the best fit.

question:
A company has an ML model. The company wants to know how the model makes predictions.
Which term refers to understanding model predictions?
explanation: A. Model interpretability → Refers to the ability to understand, explain, and trust the decisions a model makes.

question:
A company wants to identify groups for its customers based on the customers’ demographics and buying patterns.
Which algorithm should the company use to meet this requirement?
explanation: B. K-means → An unsupervised clustering algorithm that groups data points into k clusters based on similarity. Perfect for grouping customers, products, etc.

question:
A company is working on a large language model (LLM) and noticed that the LLM’s outputs are not as diverse as
expected.
Which parameter should the company adjust?
explanation:
A. Temperature → Controls the randomness of model outputs.

Lower temperature (e.g., 0.1–0.3) → More focused, deterministic answers.

Higher temperature (e.g., 0.8–1.0+) → More creative, diverse, but less predictable.

This is the parameter to adjust for style and variation in outputs.

question:
A company is using an Amazon Nova Canvas model to generate images. The model generates images successfully.
The company needs to prevent the model from including specific items in the generated images.
Which solution will meet this requirement?
explanation: C. Use a negative prompt Explicitly tells the model what not to include in the generated image (e.g., “Generate an office background, no people, no text”).

question:
A company uses ML techniques to build applications.
Select the correct ML technique from the following list for each task. Select each ML technique one time.
explanation:
Analyze a text question to determine if the answer is correct.

Task type: The output is Yes/No (Correct or Incorrect).

This is a Binary decision problem → Binary Classification.

Analyze ecological factors to determine the number of species in a certain area.

Task type: The output is a numerical value (e.g., 5 species, 17 species).

Classification tasks are for discrete classes, but here we want a continuous number prediction.

This is a Regression problem.

Analyze car attributes to determine the car model.

Task type: The output is one of multiple possible car models (e.g., Toyota, Ford, Tesla, BMW).

Since there are more than two categories, this is a Multiclass Classification problem.
items: Analyze a text question to determine if the answer is correct
Analyze ecological factors to determine the number of species in a certain area
Analyze car attributes to determine the car model

question:
A company wants to label training datasets by using human feedback to fine-tune a foundation model (FM). The
company does not want to develop labeling applications or manage a labeling workforce.
Which AWS service or feature meets these requirements?
explanation:
**B. Amazon SageMaker Ground Truth Plus**

The company wants to label datasets with human feedback.

They do not want to build labeling apps or manage a labeling workforce.

question:
An online media streaming company wants to give its customers the ability to perform natural language-based
image search and filtering. The company needs a vector database that can help with similarity searches and
nearest neighbor queries.
Which AWS service meets these requirements?
explanation:
D. Amazon OpenSearch Service.

Amazon OpenSearch Service is a distributed, open-source search and analytics suite. It includes a built-in k- Nearest Neighbor (k-NN) plugin that enables it to function as a powerful vector database.

question:
A company is building an AI solution by using Amazon SageMaker AI. The company wants to use SageMaker AI
features to facilitate application development.
Select the correct SageMaker AI feature from the following list for each use case. Select each feature one time.
explanation:
**Determine the most suitable model to use for a business case → Model Cards**

Model Cards are designed to document ML models, including performance metrics, biases, limitations, and intended use cases.

This helps business stakeholders decide whether a model is suitable for a given use case.

**Model Cards**

**Prepare data through a low-code or no-code interface → Data Wrangler**

SageMaker Data Wrangler provides a visual interface for cleaning, exploring, transforming, and preparing datasets for ML, without requiring full coding.

It saves time by offering built-in data transformations and direct integration with ML workflows.

**Data Wrangler**

**Identify biases or imbalances in the data → Clarify**

SageMaker Clarify analyzes datasets and models to detect bias and imbalances during data preparation and training.

It also provides explainability reports on how models make predictions.

**Clarify**
items: Determine the most suitable model to use for a business case
Prepare data through a low-code or no-code interface
Identify biases or imbalances in the data

question:
A company is building a generative AI tool. The company will use internal documents to customize a foundation
model (FM).
Which approach will meet this requirement?
explanation:
B. Continued pre-training.

This is when you take an existing FM and further train it on domain-specific data (in this case, the company’s internal documents).

This aligns perfectly with the requirement.

question:
A company is monitoring a predictive model by using Amazon SageMaker Model Monitor. The company notices
data drift beyond a defined threshold. The company wants to mitigate a potentially adverse impact on the
predictive model.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.C: Re-train the model with fresh data.

explanation:
C. Re-train the model with fresh data.

When Amazon SageMaker Model Monitor detects data drift, it indicates that the statistical properties of the input data have changed significantly compared to the baseline data used during the initial training. This phenomenon, often called "feature drift," typically leads to a decline in model accuracy because the model's learned patterns no longer align with the current real-world data.

question:
A financial company uses a generative AI model to assign credit limits to new customers. The company wants to
make the decision-making process of the model more transparent to its customers.
Which solution meets these requirements?

options.A: Use a rule-based system instead of an ML model.
options.B: Apply explainable AI techniques to show customers which factors influenced the model’s decision.
options.C: Develop an interactive UI for customers and provide clear technical explanations about the system.
options.D: Increase the accuracy of the model to reduce the need for transparency.

explanation:
**B. Apply explainable AI techniques**

Directly meets the requirement by showing factors that influenced predictions.

**C. Develop an interactive UI and provide technical explanations ❌**

question:
A company deployed a model to production. After 4 months, the model inference quality degraded. The company
wants to receive a notification if the model inference quality degrades. The company also wants to ensure that the
problem does not happen again.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Retrain the model. Monitor model drift by using Amazon SageMaker Clarify.
options.B: Retrain the model. Monitor model drift by using Amazon SageMaker Model Monitor.
options.C: Build a new model. Monitor model drift by using Amazon SageMaker Feature Store.
options.D: Build a new model. Monitor model drift by using Amazon SageMaker JumpStart.

explanation:
B. Retrain the model. Monitor model drift by using Amazon SageMaker Model Monitor.

This scenario describes Model Decay (or Model Drift), which occurs when a model's predictive power decreases over time because the real-world data it encounters has changed since its initial training.

question: Which option is an example of unsupervised learning?

options.A: A model that groups customers based on their purchase history
options.B: A model that classifies images as dogs or cats
options.C: A model that predicts a house’s price based on various features
options.D: A model that learns to play chess by using trial and error

explanation:
A. A model that groups customers based on their purchase history .

This is clustering, a classic unsupervised learning task.

question:
A company is evaluating several large language models (LLMs) for a text summarization task. The company needs
to select a metric to evaluate the quality of the summaries that the LLMs generate.
Which metric will meet this requirement?
explanation:
**C. ROUGE (Recall-Oriented Understudy for Gisting Evaluation)**

A set of metrics specifically designed to evaluate automatic summarization and text generation tasks.

It compares generated text with reference text using n-gram overlap, recall, and precision.

Common in NLP tasks like summarization, translation, and chatbot response quality.

question:
A research group wants to test different generative AI models to create research papers. The research group has
defined a prompt and needs a method to assess the models’ output. The research group wants to use a team of
scientists to perform the output assessments.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use automatic evaluation on Amazon Personalize.
options.B: Use content moderation on Amazon Rekognition.
options.C: Use model evaluation on Amazon Bedrock.
options.D: Use sentiment analysis on Amazon Comprehend.

explanation:
C. Use model evaluation on Amazon Bedrock.

Amazon Bedrock provides built-in model evaluation features to compare foundation models (FMs) on accuracy, relevance, and quality.

question:
An ecommerce company is developing a generative AI solution to create personalized product recommendations
for its application users. The company wants to track how effectively the AI solution increases product sales and
user engagement in the application.
Select the correct business metric from the following list for each business goal. Each business metric should be
selected one time.
explanation:
**1. Measure how engaging the product recommendations are to users**

**Answer: Click-through rate (CTR)**

CTR measures how many users click on the recommended product vs. how many times the recommendation was shown.

It is a direct engagement metric, showing if the recommendations are appealing enough to act on.

High CTR → recommendations are relevant and engaging.

**2. Determine the effect of the AI solution on the total value of user purchases**

**Answer: Average order value (AOV)**

AOV measures the average amount spent per transaction.

If recommendations are effective, users may buy more expensive items or add more items to their carts, increasing AOV.

This metric connects recommendations directly to business revenue impact.

**3. Assess the AI solution's ability to encourage users to return to the platform**

**Answer: Retention rate**

Retention rate measures the percentage of users who return to use the platform over time.

If the AI solution builds trust and satisfaction, customers are more likely to come back.

High retention means the AI is creating long-term user value, not just one-time engagement.
items: Measure how engaging the product recommendations are to users
Determine the effect of the AI solution on the total value of user purchases
Assess the AI solution's ability to encourage users to return to the platform

question:
An AI practitioner wants to evaluate ML models. The AI practitioner wants to provide explanations of model
predictions to customers and stakeholders.
Which AWS service or feature will meet these requirements?
explanation:
**Amazon SageMaker Clarify**

Amazon SageMaker Clarify is specifically designed to: Detect bias in datasets and models.

Explain model predictions using techniques like SHAP (Shapley values).

Provide transparency so stakeholders understand why the model made a certain decision.

question: Sentiment analysis is a subset of which broader field of AI?
explanation:
C. Natural language processing (NLP).

Sentiment analysis is the process of determining the emotional tone (positive, negative, neutral, or more fine- grained categories) expressed in text data.

It is a classic NLP task, since it requires understanding, processing, and classifying human language.

question:
A company wants to set up private access to Amazon Bedrock APIs from the company’s AWS account. The
company also wants to protect its data from internet exposure. Which solution meets these requirements?

options.A: Use Amazon CloudFront to restrict access to the company’s private content.
options.B: Use AWS Glue to set up data encryption across the company’s data catalog.
options.C: Use AWS Lake Formation to manage centralized data governance and cross-account data sharing.
options.D: Use AWS PrivateLink to configure a private connection between the company’s VPC and Amazon Bedrock.

explanation:
**D. Use AWS PrivateLink**

AWS PrivateLink enables private connectivity between VPCs and supported AWS services (including Amazon Bedrock).

Traffic stays on the AWS private network, not exposed to the internet.

This directly solves the requirement of securing Bedrock API access.

question:
A company receives a large amount of unstructured user feedback in text format. The company wants to analyze
the sentiment of the user feedback. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use a large language model (LLM) to perform natural language processing (NLP) for sentiment analysis.
options.B: Use a regression algorithm to classify the feedback based on predefined categories. Then, analyze user sentiment.
options.C: Use a recommendation engine algorithm to detect user sentiment.
options.D: Use a time series algorithm to predict user sentiment based on past feedback.

explanation:
A. Use an LLM for NLP sentiment analysis .

LLMs (or services like Amazon Comprehend) are designed for text understanding tasks, including sentiment analysis.

This is the most direct and effective solution.

question:
A company wants to improve multiple ML models.
Select the correct technique from the following list of use cases. Each technique should be selected one time or
not at all.
explanation:
Enhancing the capabilities of a large language model (LLM) by using external sources

**Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG)**

RAG combines an LLM with an external knowledge base (retriever).

Instead of relying only on pre-trained knowledge, it retrieves relevant documents in real time and generates answers based on both the LLM and external data.

Example: A chatbot that queries your company’s internal documents when answering.

**Querying a model to generalize and make predictions on unseen tasks**

**Zero-shot learning**

Zero-shot = the model performs tasks it has never been explicitly trained on, with no examples provided.

Relies on the model’s pre-trained knowledge and generalization ability.

Example: Asking an LLM to translate a sentence into a language it wasn’t specifically trained on.

**Querying a model with a limited amount of data for new tasks**

**Few-shot learning**

Few-shot = providing the model with a small number of labeled examples in the prompt before asking it to perform the task.

Example: Giving 3–5 examples of classifying reviews as positive/negative, then asking it to classify a new one.
items: Enhancing the capabilities of a large language model (LLM) by using external sources
Querying a model to generalize and make predictions on unseen tasks
Querying a model with a limited amount of data for new tasks

question:
A company wants to create an AI solution to generate images and descriptions for a product catalog. The company
needs to select a foundation model (FM) for this solution.
The company must consider the output types of each FM.
Which FM characteristic is the company evaluating?
explanation: D. Modality → Refers to input/output data types supported: text, image, audio, video, multimodal, etc.

question:
A company wants to use an ML model to analyze customer reviews on social media. The model must determine if
each review has a neutral, positive, or negative sentiment.
Which model evaluation strategy will meet these requirements?
explanation: D. Classification → Evaluates the model’s ability to assign data into discrete categories (e.g., spam vs. not spam, sentiment = positive/negative/neutral).

question: Select the correct AI term from the following list for each statement. Each AI term should be selected one time.
explanation:
**. Simulates human problem-solving capabilities → AI**

Artificial Intelligence (AI) is the broadest field, focused on building systems that can simulate human intelligence, such as reasoning, problem-solving, decision-making, and natural language understanding.

Examples: Chatbots, self-driving cars, expert systems.

**✅ Correct: AI**

**. Applies data-driven learning techniques to make predictions → ML**

Machine Learning (ML) is a subset of AI.

Instead of hardcoding rules, ML systems learn from data to make predictions or classifications.

Example: Predicting customer churn, fraud detection, spam classification.

**✅ Correct: ML**

**. Focuses on processing data through intricate neural networks → Deep Learning**

Deep Learning (DL) is a subset of ML.

Uses artificial neural networks with many layers ("deep") to handle complex, high-dimensional data like images, speech, and natural language.

Examples: Image recognition with CNNs, language models like GPT, voice assistants.

**✅ Correct: Deep Learning**
items: Simulates human problem-solving capabilities
Applies data-driven learning techniques to make predictions
Focuses on processing data through intricate neural networks

question: Which option is an example of unsupervised learning?

options.A: Clustering data points into groups based on their similarity
options.B: Training a model to recognize images of animals
options.C: Predicting the price of a house based on the house’s features
options.D: Generating human-like text based on a given prompt

explanation:
**A. Clustering data points into groups based on their similarity**

This is the classic unsupervised learning task.

No labels are provided — the model just groups data by similarity (e.g., customer segmentation, grouping news articles).

question:
A company is training ML models on datasets. The datasets contain some classes that have more examples than

F1 score is the harmonic mean of precision and recall.

Balances both false positives and false negatives.

Especially useful for imbalanced datasets to evaluate how well the model detects and labels all classes.

question:
A company is building a generative AI application and is reviewing foundation models (FMs). The company needs to
consider multiple FM characteristics.
Select the correct FM characteristic from the following list for each definition. Each FM characteristic should be
selected one time.
explanation:
**Amount of information that can fit in a single prompt → Context windows**

A context window is the maximum amount of text (tokens) a model can "see" and process at once in a prompt.

Larger context windows mean you can include longer documents, conversations, or instructions.

Example: GPT-4 can have a context window of up to 128K tokens, allowing it to handle very long inputs.

**2. Length of time it takes for a model to generate an output → Latency**

Latency measures the response time — how long it takes from sending a request to receiving the first token of the model’s output.

Low latency is important for real-time applications like chatbots or customer support.

High latency could result in slow user experience.

**3. Multiple users invoking an application endpoint simultaneously → Concurrency**

Concurrency is about how many requests a system can handle at the same time.

For example, if 100 users ask the AI a question at once, concurrency determines whether all requests can be processed without errors or slowdowns.

AWS services (like SageMaker or Bedrock) often have concurrency limits that you can scale.
items: Amount of information that can fit in a single prompt
Length of time it takes for a model to generate an output
Multiple users invoking an application endpoint simultaneously

question: What does inference refer to in the context of AI?

options.B: The use of a trained model to make predictions or decisions on unseen data
options.C: The process of combining multiple AI models into one model
options.D: The method of collecting training data for AI systems

explanation:

Inference is when you apply a trained model to new (unseen) input data to generate predictions, classifications, or other outputs.

question:

question:
Select and order the steps from the following list to correctly describe the ML lifecycle for a new custom model.
Select each step one time.
explanation:
**Define the business objective**

First, understand why the ML solution is being built.

Example: Predict customer churn, detect fraud, or forecast demand.

This step ensures the model aligns with real business needs.

**Process the data**

Next, gather, clean, and prepare the data.

Data preprocessing may include normalization, handling missing values, encoding categories, and splitting into training/test datasets.

Without good data, even the best models won’t perform well.

**Develop and train the model**

Apply ML algorithms to the training dataset.

Train the model, tune hyperparameters, and evaluate accuracy, precision, recall, F1 score, etc.

Example: Training a Random Forest or Neural Network on prepared data.

**Deploy the model**

Finally, put the trained model into production so it can serve predictions on new/unseen data.

question:
Select the correct prompt engineering technique from the following list for each description. Select each prompt
engineering technique one time or not at all.
explanation:
1. Provide a small number of examples to the model to understand the desired task before generating outputs.

**Selected answer: Few-shot prompting**

Explanation: Few-shot prompting involves giving the model a few examples of the task so it can infer the pattern and generate correct outputs.

Example: If you want the model to translate English sentences to French, you give 2–3 example translations, and then the model can translate a new sentence.

Key point: The model learns from examples in the prompt, not from training. 2. Prompt a model to break down the step-by-step process that the model took to arrive at a final answer.

**Selected answer: Chain-of-thought prompting**

Explanation: Chain-of-thought prompting asks the model to reason step by step rather than jumping directly to the answer.

Example: For a math problem, instead of giving only the final answer, the model explains each step: “First I added X, then I multiplied by Y…”.

Key point: This improves reasoning on complex tasks like math, logic, or multi-step reasoning. 3. Prompt a model to perform a task without providing examples.

**Selected answer: Zero-shot prompting**

Explanation: Zero-shot prompting is when you ask the model to perform a task without showing examples.

**Example: “Translate the following sentence to French: ‘Hello, how are you?’”**

The model uses its pretrained knowledge to perform the task without needing examples in the prompt.
items: Provide a small number of examples to the model to understand the desired task before generating outputs
Prompt a model to break down the step-by-step process that the model took to arrive at a final answer
Prompt a model to perform a task without providing examples

question:

options.B: Use Amazon Nova multimodal models to process the digital text and image files
options.C: Insert data into the product database

explanation:
Step 1: Upload the digital text and image files to an Amazon S3 bucket .

Amazon S3 provides scalable, secure, and cost-effective storage for objects like text and images.

Step 2: Use Amazon Nova multimodal models to process the digital text and image files .

This ensures that processed and structured data is ready for queries, reporting, or application use.

question:

question:

question:
An AI practitioner has trained a model on a training dataset. The model performs well on the training data.
However, the model does not perform well on evaluation data.
What is the MOST likely cause of this issue?
explanation:

Overfitting occurs when a model performs very well on training data but fails to generalize to new or unseen data. This is the most common cause when a model gives poor predictions in real-world scenarios despite seemingly strong training performance.

question:
A company is using Amazon Bedrock to build an AI assistant. The AI assistant helps customers find relevant
products by making suggestions. However, the AI assistant's responses are often generic and irrelevant. The
company wants to use prompt engineering to improve the AI assistant's responses.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use few-shot prompting to add domain-specific context and explicit instructions.
options.B: Use chain-of-thought prompting with hidden reasoning steps to ignore explicit domain instructions.
options.C: Modify the AI assistant's conversational style to use more formal language and include technical product specifications.
options.D: Use zero-shot prompting to augment retrieval from a product database.

explanation: Few-shot prompting solves this by: •Providing domain-specific examples (e.g., “Given this user inquiry, suggest relevant products.”) •Teaching the model the pattern of correct responses •Anchoring the assistant to specific context (e.g., product category, features, user intent) Few-shot prompting is the most effective prompt-engineering technique for improving quality, relevance, and domain alignment.

question:

question:

question:

question:
A company wants to build generative AI applications by using Amazon Bedrock. The company wants to minimize
development effort.
Select and order the model development techniques from the following list from the LEAST development effort to
the MOST development effort. Each model development technique should be selected one time.
explanation:
**1(cid:3) ⃣ Prompt engineering (cid:93) Least development effort**

What it is: You modify only the input prompts (instructions, examples, system messages) given to a pre-trained model.

Why it requires the least effort:

**No model training**

**No new datasets**

**No infrastructure changes**

**Can be done immediately via API or UI**

Typical use cases:

**Improving response format**

**Adding step-by-step reasoning**

**Enforcing tone, style, or constraints**

**Fastest, cheapest, lowest risk**

**2(cid:3) ⃣ Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG)**

What it is: The model remains unchanged, but it retrieves relevant documents (from a vector database or search index) and uses them as context during generation.

Why it requires more effort than prompt engineering: Requires building:

**Document ingestion pipeline**

**Embeddings**

**Vector database or search service**

**Still no model retraining**

Typical use cases:

**Using private or up-to-date data**

**Enterprise knowledge bases**

**Reducing hallucinations**

**Moderate system complexity, low model complexity**

**3(cid:3) ⃣ Fine-tuning**

What it is: You train the model further on a labeled dataset to change its behavior or specialization.

Why effort is higher: Requires:

**Curated training data**

**Training jobs**

**Evaluation and monitoring**

**Risk of overfitting or bias**

**Ongoing maintenance**

Typical use cases:

**Domain-specific language**

**Consistent structured outputs**

**Task-specific performance gains**

**Higher cost and engineering effort**

**4(cid:3) ⃣ Continued pre-training (cid:93) Most development effort**

What it is: Training a model further on massive amounts of raw (often unlabeled) data to change its underlying knowledge.

Why it requires the most effort: Requires:

**Very large datasets**

**Significant compute (GPUs/TPUs)**

**Deep ML expertise**

**Long training cycles**

**High infrastructure and operational cost**

Typical use cases:

**Adapting foundation models to new languages**

**Industry-scale model development**

**Fundamental knowledge expansion**

**Highest cost, highest complexity**

question:
An airline company wants to use a generative AI model to convert a flight booking system from one coding
language into another coding language. The company must select a model for this task.
Which criteria should the company use to select the correct generative AI model for this task?

options.A: Syntax, semantic understanding, and code optimization capabilities
options.B: Code generation speed and error handling capabilities

explanation:
**A. Syntax, semantic understanding, and code optimization capabilities**

To convert code from one programming language to another, the model must:

**Understand syntax of both source and target languages**

**Understand semantics (logic, data flow, functionality)**

**Optimize or maintain code quality during translation**

These capabilities are essential for accurate and reliable code translation, which is a highly technical and structured task.

Generative AI models built for code tasks (e.g., CodeWhisperer, Code Llama, GPT-Code models) are evaluated primarily on these criteria.

question:
An AI practitioner is using Amazon Bedrock Prompt Management to create a reusable prompt. The prompt must be
able to interact with external services by calling an external API.
Which solution will meet this requirement?
explanation:
**B. Use a tools configuration**

In Amazon Bedrock Prompt Management, if you want a prompt to:

**Call external APIs**

**Interact with external services**

**Trigger specific tools during generation**

…you must configure Tools (also called tool use or function calling).

A tools configuration lets the model call external functions or APIs in a structured, reliable way—just like function calling in modern LLM frameworks.

question:
A company wants to use Amazon Q Business for its data. The company needs to ensure the security and privacy of
the data.
Which combination of steps will meet these requirements? (Choose two.)

options.A: Enable AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) keys for the Amazon Q Business Enterprise index.
options.B: Set up cross-account access to the Amazon Q index.
options.D: Allow public access to the Amazon Q index.
options.E: Configure AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) for authentication.

explanation:
A. Enable AWS KMS keys for the Amazon Q Business Enterprise index.

Ensures encryption at rest, protecting sensitive company data.

Amazon Q Business supports customer-managed KMS keys.

E. Configure AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) for authentication.

IAM ensures secure access control, governing who can access Q Business and what they can perform.

This is essential for privacy and security.

question:
A company uses Amazon Comprehend to analyze customer feedback. A customer has several unique trained
models. The company uses Comprehend to assign each model an endpoint. The company wants to automate a
report on each endpoint that is not used for more than 15 days.
Which service will meet these requirements?
explanation:
B. Amazon CloudWatch.

Amazon Comprehend endpoints automatically publish invocation metrics to Amazon CloudWatch, including:

**InvocationCount**

**4XX/5XX errors**

**Latency**

To detect endpoints not used for 15+ days, you can: Create a CloudWatch metric alarm that triggers when InvocationCount = 0 over a 15-day period.

Use CloudWatch Events / EventBridge to generate a report or send notifications.

This directly measures usage and works perfectly for the requirement.

question:
A company plans to use a generative AI model to provide real-time service quotes to users.
Which criteria should the company use to select the correct model for this use case?

options.C: General-purpose use and high-powered GPU availability
options.D: Model latency and optimized inference speed

explanation:
**D. Model latency and optimized inference speed**

The company needs a generative AI model that can provide real-time service quotes, meaning:

**Low latency (fast responses)**

**High throughput**

**Optimized inference performance**

question:
An AI practitioner must fine-tune an open source large language model (LLM) for text categorization. The dataset
is already prepared.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational effort?

options.A: Create a custom model training job in PartyRock on Amazon Bedrock.
options.B: Use Amazon SageMaker JumpStart to create a training job.
options.C: Use a custom script to run an Amazon SageMaker AI model training job.
options.D: Create a Jupyter notebook on an Amazon EC2 instance. Use the notebook to train the model.

explanation:
**B. Use Amazon SageMaker JumpStart to create a training job**

SageMaker JumpStart provides:

**Prebuilt notebooks and workflows**

**1-click training and fine-tuning for many open-source LLMs**

**Managed infrastructure (no need to configure training clusters manually)**

**Minimal setup and operational overhead**

It is specifically designed to fine-tune open-source models with very low operational effort.

This makes it the easiest and most efficient option.

question: What is the primary purpose of system prompts in generative AI applications?

options.A: To authenticate user credentials to access responses from AI
options.B: To generate random responses by AI
options.C: To define the role and behavioral boundaries of AI
options.D: To compress output data from AI

explanation:
**Technical justification**

**Correct option – C: “To define the role and behavioral boundaries of AI.”**

System prompts are the initial instructions that steer the model’s behavior, establishing its persona, scope, and constraints (e.g., “You are a helpful support assistant”). This shapes how the model interprets subsequent user inputs and ensures consistent, safe, and purpose-aligned outputs.

**Why the other choices are incorrect**

A. Authentication of user credentials – Authentication is handled by security mechanisms (IAM, Cognito, etc.), not by system prompts. Prompts do not verify identity.

B. Generation of random responses – Randomness is controlled by sampling parameters (temperature, top-p), not by the system prompt. Prompts guide, not randomize, responses.

D. Compression of output data – Output size optimization is achieved through token limits, truncation, or streaming, not by crafting system prompts.

**Conclusion**

System prompts serve as the behavioral contract between the developer and the model, making option C the only statement that accurately captures their primary purpose.

**References**

AWS Documentation – Prompt Engineering for Amazon Bedrock: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/bedrock-prompt-engineering.html AWS Documentation – System Prompts and Model Behavior: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/system-prompts.html

question:
A company is using a large language model (LLM) to create a generative AI assistant. The company must choose
an AI technique to ensure that the AI assistant generates the most factually correct responses. The company
selects the Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) technique.
Which limitation of LLMs is the company trying to reduce?
explanation:
The company is targeting hallucinations – a core limitation of LLMs where the model generates plausible-sounding but factually incorrect or fabricated information. By employing Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), the assistant first retrieves relevant, up-to-date documents from an external knowledge source and then grounds the response in that retrieved content. This retrieval step anchors the generation process in verified facts, dramatically lowering the probability of the model inventing details that are not present in the source material. Consequently, RAG directly mitigates the hallucination issue, enhancing the factual reliability of the AI assistant’s outputs.

Why A (Hallucinations) is the best choice: RAG reduces hallucinations by coupling a retrieval component that supplies factual context to the generative component, ensuring that generated text is more tightly tied to real, external data.

Why B (Security) is not the focus: Security concerns (e.g., injection attacks, data leakage) relate to how the system is protected, not to the factual accuracy of the output. RAG does not inherently address security risks.

Why C (Nondeterminism) is not the primary limitation here: While LLMs can be nondeterministic, RAG’s main benefit is not about making responses deterministic; it is about improving factual grounding.

Why D (Interpretability) is not directly resolved: RAG does not make the model’s internal reasoning more transparent or explainable; it merely supplies external evidence to guide generation.

**References**

AWS Documentation – Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) Overview: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/rag.html Amazon Bedrock – Using RAG with Knowledge Bases: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/knowledge-bases.html

question:
A company is building a job recommendation system based on job posting data and job seeker user profiles. The
system shows bias in job recommendations based on gender for user profiles that are otherwise equivalent.
Which principle should the company follow to address this issue, according to AWS best practices for responsible
AI?
explanation:
**Technical justification**

Fairness is the AWS responsibility pillar that directly targets systematic bias in AI outputs.

In a job-recommendation model, gender-based discrimination indicates a failure to treat equivalent user profiles equitably.

Applying the Fairness principle means auditing training data, selecting unbiased features, and using techniques such as re-weighting, adversarial debiasing, or fairness-aware evaluation metrics to ensure predictions do not disadvantage any protected group.

This principle aligns with AWS Responsible AI guidance, which emphasizes “ensuring that AI systems produce equitable outcomes for all users” and provides best-practice checklists for bias mitigation.

**Why the other options are less suitable**

Governance focuses on oversight processes, policies, and stakeholder accountability; it is essential but does not directly address the technical bias observed in recommendations.

Explainability concerns understanding why a model makes a particular prediction; while useful for debugging, it does not by itself prevent unfair outcomes.

Controllability relates to steering model behavior (e.g., via prompts or parameters) without guaranteeing that the underlying outputs are fair.

Thus, the principle that directly resolves the bias issue is Fairness (option D).

**References**

**AWS Responsible AI Principles: https://aws.amazon.com/responsible-ai/**

AWS Guide to Reducing Bias in Machine Learning: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/machine-learning/latest/dg/biased-data.html

question:
A company wants its AI models to be transparent and explainable.
Which combination of Amazon SageMaker AI features will meet these requirements? (Choose two.)
explanation:
**Why the correct combination (A + C) satisfies the requirement**

SageMaker Model Cards (A) – Provides a standardized, human-readable documentation artifact that captures a model’s provenance, intended use, training data, evaluation metrics, and fairness assessments. By exposing this information, stakeholders can audit the model’s design choices and understand its limitations, directly supporting transparency.

SageMaker Clarity (C) – Delivers visual, model-agnostic explanations such as feature importance, partial dependence plots, and SHAP-based insights. These explanations make the model’s decision-making process interpretable to both technical and non-technical audiences, fulfilling the explainability criterion.

**Why the other options are not appropriate for this specific need**

SageMaker Pipelines (B) – Primarily orchestrates end-to-end ML workflows (data ingestion, training, deployment). It does not provide mechanisms for model documentation or interpretability.

SageMaker Model Monitor (D) – Focuses on detecting data drift, model decay, and performance degradation after deployment. While useful for operational monitoring, it does not address transparency or explainability of the model itself.

SageMaker Debugger (E) – Captures intermediate tensors and debugging metrics during training to help diagnose issues like vanishing gradients or overfitting. It aids model development but does not generate explanations or documentation for end-users.

Therefore, the pair A (Model Cards) and C (Clarity) uniquely combines formal model documentation with interactive, visual explanations, meeting both transparency and explainability requirements.

**References**

SageMaker Model Cards – https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/latest/dg/model-cards.html

**SageMaker Clarity – https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/latest/dg/clarity.html**

question:
A company is developing a product recommendation application by using a generative AI model. The company
must minimize the application’s environmental impact.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Optimize the deployed model architecture to prioritize computational efficiency during model inference.
options.B: Adopt a distributed inference approach by using multiple smaller models across multiple Availability Zones.
options.C: Adopt a hybrid strategy by deploying the model on premises and storing the data on AWS.
options.D: Deploy multiple models and use a dynamic model selection mechanism that queries different models randomly.

explanation:
**Why option A is the optimal solution**

Computational efficiency directly reduces energy use – Optimizing model architecture (e.g., pruning, quantization, using smaller embedding dimensions) minimizes the number of arithmetic operations required during inference, which translates into lower CPU/GPU cycles, reduced power draw, and a smaller carbon footprint per recommendation request.

**AWS-native sustainability guidance – AWS recommends “right-sizing” AI workloads**

(https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/machine-learning/optimizing-models-for-cost-and-efficiency/) and prioritizes inference-level optimizations (https://aws.amazon.com/solutions/generative-ai/ai-sustainability-best- practices/) before scaling infrastructure. Option A aligns with this principle.

Simplicity and maintainability – A single optimized model is easier to version, test, and monitor, avoiding the operational overhead of managing many services or distributed pipelines.

**Why the other options are less suitable**

B. Distributed inference with multiple smaller models across AZs – While distribution can improve latency, it actually increases total compute resources (multiple model copies) and network traffic, raising overall energy consumption and operational complexity.

C. Hybrid on-premises deployment with data stored on AWS – Running part of the workload on-premises introduces additional hardware that may be less energy-efficient than AWS’s purpose-built, carbon-aware infrastructure, and it complicates data-flow governance.

D. Random dynamic model selection – Querying different models at random adds unpredictable compute demands, making it impossible to guarantee bounded energy usage and leading to higher average resource consumption.

Therefore, optimizing the deployed model architecture for computational efficiency (Option A) best aligns with minimizing environmental impact while maintaining functional performance.

**References**

Optimizing ML models for cost and efficiency – AWS Machine Learning Blog: https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/machine-learning/optimizing-models-for-cost-and-efficiency/ AI sustainability best practices on AWS – AWS Solutions Library: https://aws.amazon.com/solutions/generative-ai/ai-sustainability-best-practices/

question:
A company uses Amazon SageMaker AI to generate article summaries in multiple languages. The company needs
a metric to evaluate the quality of the summary translations in multiple languages.
Which evaluation metric will meet these requirements?
explanation:
**Why BLEU is the appropriate choice**

Bleu (Bilingual Evaluation Understudy) is specifically designed for evaluating the quality of generated text that is a translation or summary across languages. It compares the candidate output with references using n-gram precision, weighting matches by how common the phrase is in the reference, and therefore captures both lexical accuracy and fluency in multilingual contexts.

ROUGE focuses on recall of overlapping n-grams and is primarily used for automatic summarization evaluation, but it is less sensitive to translation fluency and does not account for precision weighting as explicitly as BLEU.

AUC measures the separability of two classes (e.g., binary classification) and is unrelated to assessing textual quality in translation or summarization tasks.

Precision alone provides only a single numeric score without reference comparison, offering no insight into how well the generated text aligns with fluent, target-language references.

Conclusion: BLEU meets the requirement of evaluating multi-language summary translations by providing a standardized, reference-based metric that balances precision and fluency across languages, making it the best answer.

**References**

BLEU (Bilingual Evaluation Understudy) – Official documentation: https://www.aclweb.org/anthology/P05-1040.pdf Amazon SageMaker model evaluation best practices (BLEU usage): https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/latest/dg/model-evaluation.html

question:
A research company is growing microbiological cultures. The company captures images of the cultures without
any prior labeled data about growth areas. The company needs to identify the regions of the images that show
culture growth.
Which ML technique will meet these requirements?
explanation:
**Why clustering (Option C) fits the problem**

The task is to discover and segment regions within unlabeled microscope images that exhibit microbial growth.

No ground-truth labels are available, so the algorithm must infer structure from raw pixel data.

Clustering groups similar pixels/regions by their feature characteristics (e.g., color, texture, intensity) without requiring prior labels, making it ideal for unsupervised region discovery.

Once clusters are formed, each cluster can be interpreted as a “growth region,” enabling subsequent analysis or visualization.

**Why the other options are unsuitable**

Logistic regression (A) and Decision tree (B) are supervised methods; they need labeled examples to learn a decision boundary, which the scenario explicitly lacks.

Dimensionality reduction (D) (e.g., PCA, t-SNE) transforms data to a lower-dimensional space but does not create discrete regions or classes; it is typically a preprocessing step rather than a complete solution for identifying growth areas.

Bottom line: Only clustering provides an unsupervised way to partition image data into meaningful regions based on similarity, matching the requirement to locate unlabeled growth areas.

**References**

AWS Machine Learning Foundations – Unsupervised Learning: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/machine-learning/latest/deep-learning-framework/unordered.html Amazon SageMaker Built-In Algorithms – K-means: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/latest/dgbuilt-in-algorithms.html#k-means-algorithm

question:
A company that streams media is selecting an Amazon Nova foundation model (FM) to process documents and
images. The company is comparing Nova Micro and Nova Lite. The company wants to minimize costs.
Which model characteristics should the company consider to meet these requirements?

options.A: Nova Micro uses transformer-based architectures. Nova Lite does not use transformer-based architectures.
options.B: Nova Micro supports only text data. Nova Lite is optimized for numerical data.
options.C: Nova Micro supports only text. Nova Lite supports images, videos, and text.
options.D: Nova Micro runs only on CPUs. Nova Lite runs only on GPUs.

explanation:
**Justification**

**Option C correctly reflects the multimodal capabilities of the two models**

Nova Micro is designed for text-only inference, whereas Nova Lite extends the foundation model to handle images, videos, and text in addition to multimodal reasoning.

Because the streaming company needs to process both documents (text) and images, they must choose a model that supports visual input, which only Nova Lite provides.

**Why the other options are incorrect**

A – Both Nova Micro and Nova Lite are built on transformer architectures; there is no architectural exclusion in Nova Lite.

B – Neither model is limited to “numerical data”; both can ingest textual input, and Nova Lite also processes visual modalities.

D – Deployment hardware is not model-specific in this way; both models can run on CPUs or GPUs depending on the deployment configuration, so the statement mischaracterizes their runtime capabilities.

**Cost-minimization rationale**

Since Nova Lite can handle the broader data types required (images, videos, and text) while still offering a relatively low-cost inference footprint compared to larger multimodal models, it satisfies the company’s requirement to minimize costs without sacrificing the necessary multimodal capability.

Conclusion: The company should consider that Nova Micro supports only text, whereas Nova Lite supports images, videos, and text, making option C the most appropriate choice.

**References**

Amazon Nova Technical Overview – Text & Image Models: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/nova/latest/multimodal/overview.html Choosing the right AWS AI model for your workload – AWS Blog: https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/machine-learning/choosing-the-right-aws-ai-model-for-your-workload/

question:
A company stores customer data in OpenSearch. The company wants an AI solution to retrieve specific customer
information from the stored data. The AI solution must convert queries into data requests and generate CSV files
from the results. Then, the AI solution must upload the CSV files to Amazon S3.
Which solution will meet these requirements in the MOST operationally-efficient way?

options.A: Create an AI agent to perform the required steps.
options.B: Use a single foundation model (FM) with few-shot prompting.
options.C: Create a software application without using AI to perform the required steps.
options.D: Train a decision tree model to generate a solution based on user questions.

explanation:
**Why Option A is the best choice**

Orchestrated AI agent – An AI-powered agent (e.g., an Amazon Bedrock Agent or a custom Lambda-triggered workflow) can interpret natural-language queries, translate them into OpenSearch requests, format the returned results as CSV, and invoke an S3 PUT operation—all in a single, state-ful execution.

Operational efficiency – Managed services (Bedrock, Lambda, S3) take care of scaling, patching, and monitoring, so the company does not need to maintain bespoke infrastructure or write repetitive code.

Reusability – The same agent can be extended to support additional data sources or transformations without redesigning a separate application.

Consistency & auditability – Errors (e.g., malformed queries) can be caught early by the agent’s validation layer, and logs/c all be centrally collected for compliance.

**Why the other options are less suitable**
